codeReview.summary: The fix adds the missing regression coverage for `assistant_response`, `report_image_captured`, `report_images_selection`, and `session_tabs_update`, but the feature still fails review because commit `58ec278` again bundles unrelated package/manifest version bumps instead of being a test-only repair.

issues.description: Commit `58ec278` bumps the package version from `0.5.8` to `0.5.9` even though this fix is only supposed to add the missing runtime-message regression tests. That repeats the same release-metadata scope problem flagged in the original review instead of resolving it.
The same commit also bumps the Chrome manifest version from `0.5.8` to `0.5.9`, which is unrelated shipped metadata and outside the scope of the runtime-message coverage fix.
The Firefox manifest version is likewise bumped from `0.5.8` to `0.5.9` in this test-coverage fix commit, so the fix still carries the original feature's versioning side effect.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared-state docs are still inconsistent about runtime-message structure: `shared-foundation-notes.md` says the entrypoint is `runtime-messages.ts` with 22 variants, but the architecture/library docs and the mission validation contract still describe `runtime-message-definitions.ts` and 21 variants.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/shared-foundation-notes.md:1 says `packages/shared/src/runtime-messages.ts` is the 22-variant entrypoint; `.factory/library/architecture.md:31-32,95` and `/Users/sero/.factory/missions/fdd24bf3-fc74-45b3-a646-4179615c1e31/validation-contract.md:10` still reference `runtime-message-definitions.ts` and 21 variants.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `refactor-worker` skill's documented final validation procedure still does not match what fix workers are recording as compliant. This fix handoff says `followedProcedure: true` after recording only typecheck, unit tests, and repo-standards via pre-commit, while the skill still requires integration, lint, and build in its final validation section.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/refactor-worker/SKILL.md:94-110 requires `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, `npm run test:integration`, `npm run lint`, `npm run check:repo-standards`, and `npm run build`; handoff `2026-03-11T00-53-11-786Z__fix-runtime-messages-test-coverage__497d5cd6-904b-45cd-975d-843eaef00bbd.json`:16-26,68 records only `npm run typecheck`, `npm run test:unit`, and `npm run check:repo-standards` and still sets `followedProcedure` to true.

summary: The new tests at `tests/unit/suites/runtime-messages.test.ts:208-364` do close the original serialization/deserialization coverage gap for the four missing runtime-message variants declared in `packages/shared/src/runtime-messages-base.ts:85` and `packages/shared/src/runtime-messages-extended.ts:102,124,141`. However, fix commit `58ec278` again bundles unrelated package/manifest version bumps, so this re-review still fails as a self-contained repair for the original scrutiny findings.
